PROCEDURE: ORIF Symphysis

 

Findings: Anterior mandibular fracture with soft tissue ingrowth identified. Fracture accessed via anterior mandibular vestibular incision. Mental nerves not visualized but protected. Fracture reduced and fixated with a [#]-hole [profile] Biomet plate at the inferior border and a [profile] Biomet tension plate superiorly. Maxillomandibular fixation (MMF) placed and released. Stable, repeatable occlusion confirmed.

 

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under general anesthesia and intubated via a nasal endotracheal route without incident. The patient was then draped per sterile protocol. The patient was given 2g ancef IV prior to incision.

 

Attention was then directed to the oral cavity. The oral cavity was suctioned free of secretions and debris and a throat pack was placed. 10 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100K epi was infiltrated into the maxillary and mandibular vestibules. Using a 24 G wire, a bridle wire was placed from tooth [#__] to level the mandibular occlusion. Maxillary and mandibular arch bars were placed with 24G wire from 2nd pre-molar to 2nd pre-molar. The arches were then placed into MMF. Occlusion was verified.

 

Attention was then drawn to the anterior mandibular vestibule. Using a #15 blade, an incision was made 15 mm anterior to the MGJ from [#__]. The incision extended to the mandibular bone. Using a periosteal elevator, a subperiosteal dissection was completed exposing the entire fracture and the inferior border. The mental nerves were not visualized. The soft tissue ingrowth was then removed at the fracture site. The site was copiously irrigated. Using a 702 bur, two osteotomies were placed to engage bone reduction forceps to achieve proper reduction of the fracture. A [#]-hole, [profile] Biomet plate was placed at the inferior border with [#] screws. In addition, a [profile] Biomet plate was placed as a tension plate superior to the previous plate with [#] screws.

 

The mandible was observed to be stable. Copious irrigation was used. The mentalis muscle was reapproximated with 3-0 vicryl sutures and the mucosa was closed with 3-0 vicryl in a running fashion. The patient was released from MMF and his occlusion was stable and repeatable in a passive fashion.

 

The patient’s oropharynx was then irrigated and suctioned free of debris and throat pack removed. The patient was then awakened and extubated without incidence. The patient was transported to the PACU for recovery and was noted to have stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations on arrival.
